word_list.pronunciation: lˈɪɾəl pˈɪtʃɚz tˈɛnd tʊ hæv bˈɪɡ lˈɑːɹdʒ ˈɪɹz

senses.definition: used to caution adults to be mindful of what they say and do around children, as they may be more aware and perceptive than they appear

examples.x: When discussing sensitive or private matters, it's best to do so in a private setting, as little pitchers have big ears and they may unintentionally repeat what they hear.

word_list.word: the day has eyes, the night has ears
word_list.pronunciation: ðə dˈeɪ hɐz ˈaɪz ðə nˈaɪt hɐz ˈɪɹz

senses.definition: used to advise that people should be aware of their surroundings and actions, as they may be observed or overheard

word_list.word: (eavesdroppers|listeners) never hear any good of themselves
word_list.pronunciation: ˈiːvzdɹɑːpɚz lˈɪsənɚz nˈɛvɚ hˈɪɹ ˌɛni ɡˈʊd ʌv ðɛmsˈɛlvz

senses.definition: used to imply that if someone listens in on conversations that were not meant for their ears, they are likely to hear unfavorable things being said about themselves

examples.x: When Jack overheard his coworkers talking about him, he realized that eavesdroppers never hear any good of themselves.

senses.definition: used to suggest that people should respect the skills and expertise of others and not interfere in their work, but rather allow them to do their job to the best of their ability

examples.x: When the architect designed the building, the contractor followed the plans, recognizing that every man to his trade and he was not qualified to make design decisions.

word_list.word: let every fox take care of his own tail
word_list.pronunciation: lˈɛt ˈɛvɹi fˈɑːks tˈeɪk kˈɛɹ ʌv hɪz ˈoʊn tˈeɪl

senses.definition: used to suggest that individuals should not interfere in the affairs of others, but rather focus on their own lives and well-being

examples.x: When the neighbors argued over their property lines, the mediator advised them to let every fox take care of his own tail and not to interfere with each other's property.

senses.definition: used to suggest that individuals should take care of their own problems and faults before criticizing or attempting to correct others
used to imply that one should be careful about what is said in private, as there may be others listening or eavesdropping

examples.x: The spies were cautious about their conversations, knowing that walls have ears and any information leaked could be used against them.
